_metadata.description: Banned and recalled ingredients database for regulatory compliance and safety (v3.0)

v5_repurposed_fields.status: Changed from free-text to enum: banned|recalled|high_risk|watchlist

legal_status_enum.banned_federal: Federal FDA/DEA ban - illegal to sell
legal_status_enum.not_lawful_as_supplement: Drug exclusion rule or not a dietary ingredient
legal_status_enum.restricted: Legal with restrictions in some jurisdictions

status.banned: FDA ban, controlled substance, drug exclusion -> FAIL
status.recalled: FDA recall, specific product scope -> FAIL
status.high_risk: FDA warning letter, NDI rejection, contaminant -> -10
status.watchlist: International bans, restricted use, FDA monitoring -> -5

match_mode.historical: Entry kept for reference but no longer matched

changes: Migrated from category arrays to single ingredients[] list
Added legal_status_enum and clinical_risk_enum separation
Added jurisdictions[] with ISO codes
Added match_rules with priority and negative terms
Added references_structured with evidence grades
Deduplicated Sibutramine (SPIKE_SIBUTRAMINE merged)
Fixed status contradictions for high_risk_ingredients
Production hardening: added schema_mappings block
Enhanced jurisdictions with jurisdiction_code (ISO)
Enhanced match_rules with priority, confidence, negative_match_terms
Enhanced references_structured with supports_claims
Added governance metadata
Added 1 UMLS CUI identifiers
Added 9 PubChem CID identifiers
Added 13 CAS numbers
Added 1 FDA UNII identifiers
Added identifier notes for 38 product/category entries
Full data quality audit: 8 stub mechanisms replaced with clinically accurate text
2 short notes expanded (BANNED_DMBA, BANNED_PICAMILON)
12 class entries set to match_mode=disabled (matcher skips entity_type=class)
Added SPIKE_METHOCARBAMOL (prescription muscle relaxant spiking agent)
Alias-token bidirectional sync completed (192 aliases added in earlier chunks)
13 clinical_risk_enum mismatches resolved
4 severity upgrades (bitter orange, yohimbe, tramadol, delta-8 THC)
Total entries: 127 → 128
Added regulatory_date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD) to all 128 entries — single UX-facing date field
Added regulatory_date_label (human label) to all 128 entries
61 entries: copied from existing banned_date
20 entries: extracted from references_structured
47 entries: researched from FDA, DEA, WADA, Prop 65, EFSA primary sources
Enricher patched to pass regulatory_date + regulatory_date_label to scored output
Migrated legacy effective_date/ban_effective_date into effective_period.start then removed legacy fields
Migrated prohibition_status into regulatory_actions scope for WADA entries then removed prohibition_status
Migrated banned_countries into jurisdictions country rows then removed banned_countries
Migrated contraindications into population_warnings and interaction rules then removed contraindications
Added RULE_BANNED_7KETO_DHEA_PREGNANCY to ingredient_interaction_rules
removed drop-now redundant fields from banned_recalled entries

fields removed: recent_recalls_2025, safe_dose_limit, ca_prop65_limit_mcg_day, chemical_symbol, fda_limit_mcg_day, health_effects, high_risk_sources, usp_limit_mcg_day, exposure_context, jurisdictional_statuses, update_flags, regulatory_status_enum, scientific_references
Schema 5.0 migration: removed 27+ dead/redundant fields per entry
Repurposed status to 4-bucket model: banned, recalled, high_risk, watchlist
Added top-level match_mode field: active, disabled, historical
Added recall_scope for recalled product entries
Fixed 21 jurisdiction entries with wrong country codes
Moved 6 disabled dye entries out of BR (already scored via HA)
Moved 7 FDA-approved additive entries from BR to HA
Moved 14 entries with regulatory action from HA to BR
Downgraded 3 entries (potassium bromate, citrus red 2, orange B) to watchlist
Set RECALLED_HYDROXYCUT to match_mode=historical
Updated enricher with match_mode gate and status-to-severity derivation
Updated scorer with status-based B0 logic (4-bucket model)
Total entries: 129 -> 130

known_limitations: State bans may not be exhaustive - new state legislation enacted frequently
Some synthetic compounds lack UMLS CUI identifiers
Dose thresholds are approximations based on available literature
Match rules for botanicals may have false positive/negative edge cases
data_quality_notes: All entries validated against FDA, DEA, and state regulatory databases
References with evidence_grade 'R' are regulatory sources
References with evidence_grade 'A' are RCTs or meta-analyses
Entries marked needs_review require additional source verification

migration_notes: Migrated from v2.2 category-based structure to single ingredients[] list
Added legal_status_enum and clinical_risk_enum for accurate classification
Added match_rules for deterministic ingredient matching
Added jurisdictions for state/regional ban support
